read: Registered/zero-copy combine input is shape-dependent. It only removes a tiny prepare/copy stage, but the MORI combine kernel behavior changes substantially.
EP4 rejects registered mode: step2 steady combine_dx.call regresses to ~12.68 ms from the external runtime baseline ~4.56 ms median.

EP8 is the interesting target shape: both direct GBS8 A/B runs passed, and registered mode improves combine_dx.call median from ~40.63 ms to ~28.15 ms when excluding the first call of step 2, with only small local-VJP median regressions.

The required no-timing GBS128 follow-up passed in run_artifacts/torchtitan_dsv4_mori_registered_neltsruntime_8x_s4096_gbs128_20260603.json: registered mode reaches 3026 tok/GPU/s versus the retained external-mode 2936 tok/GPU/s baseline, so it is the current retained EP8 canary setting.

result.read: runtime n_elements external-buffer event-timing baseline from mori_eventtiming_ep4_comparison_20260603

scope: A/B TORCHTITAN_MORI_AITER_BACKWARD_COMBINE_INPUT_MODE=external vs registered for MORI routed backward combine_dx at S4096 EP4/EP8 one-accumulation diagnostic shapes.
